mechanism: Aescin is a mixture of triterpene saponins that stimulates endothelial nitric oxide synthesis and increases endothelial cell permeability to calcium, promoting release of prostaglandin F2α; it also antagonizes serotonin and histamine and reduces degradation of mucopolysaccharides, resulting in vasoprotective, anti-inflammatory and venotonic effects.
contraindications: Individuals with bleeding disorders or taking anticoagulants should avoid use
May interfere with blood sugar regulation; caution in diabetics
May cause mild nausea or abdominal upset
Pregnant or breastfeeding individuals should consult healthcare provider before use
safetyNotes: high doses can be toxic due to aescin
